alternates.evidence: Faucets and shelves should be of contrasting colour to the surfaces to which they are attached.

alternates.evidence: The main waiting area shall be designed to allow a person using a wheelchair to turn in an open space not less than 1 700 mm in diameter.

alternates.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
alternates.description: Service counters used by the general public must be accessible, including ticket counters, refreshment stands, drinking fountains, cafeteria counters, check-out counters, and bank service counters.
alternates.evidence: service counters used by the general public (e.g., ticket counters, refreshment stands, drinking fountains, cafeteria counters, check-out counters, bank service counters).

alternates.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
alternates.description: Push button operators for power-assisted doors must be mounted 900–1,100 mm high for ease of access and use.
alternates.evidence: The push button operators for the power-assisted door should be mounted 900 mm - 1 100 mm high for ease of access and use.

alternates.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
alternates.description: Every counter over 2 m long serving the public must have at least one barrier-free section not less than 760 mm long, centered over a knee space.
alternates.evidence: Every counter more than 2 m long, at which the public is served, shall have at least one barrier-free section not less than 760 mm long centred over a knee space

alternates.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
alternates.description: Barrier-free counter surface must not be more than 865 mm above the floor.
alternates.evidence: A barrier-free counter surface shall not be more than 865 mm above the floor.

alternates.label: Accessible Reception and Counters

alternates.evidence: the knee space beneath a barrier-free counter...shall not be less than a) 760 mm wide, b) 685 mm high, and c) 485 mm deep.

alternates.label: Accessible Reception and Counters

alternates.evidence: Banks shall also conform to Sentences (2) and (3) when designing service counters for clients who may use a wheelchair or other mobility device...The same standards should be applied when installing accessible ATMs

alternates.label: Accessible Reception and Counters

alternates.evidence: A barrier-free washroom shall be provided with a lavatory that ... has a rim height not more than 865 mm above the ﬂ oor

alternates.evidence:
Toilet paper dispenser: 750 mm–850 mm
b) Paper towel dispenser or hand dryer: 1 100 mm–1 200 mm
c) Soap: 900 mm–1 000 mm
d) Other dispensers: 1 100 mm–1 200 mm

alternates.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
alternates.description: Entryways, kitchens, washrooms, laundry rooms and other areas of a dwelling unit shall be designed with an unobstructed turning diameter of not less than 1 500 mm.

values.text: turning diameter of not less than 1 500 mm

alternates.evidence: Every kitchen counter shall have at least one barrier-free section not less than 760 mm long...top surface...not more than 865 mm above the finished floor.

alternates.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
alternates.description: At least 20% of tables and no fewer than one in outdoor public use eating areas must be accessible.
alternates.evidence: ensure a minimum of twenty percent (20%) of tables and no fewer than one (1) in outdoor public use eating area are accessible;

alternates.evidence: accessible washrooms and stalls, access to paper towels and soap dispensers, lever handles, larger handles on locking devices

alternates.label: Accessible Reception and Counters

alternates.evidence: The level of illumination at the car controls, platform, car threshold, and landing sill shall be 100 lx minimum.

alternates.description: Counters and work surfaces must be installed so they can be replaced with accessible versions without modifying structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems.
alternates.evidence: Counters and work surfaces shall: a) be installed in a manner that their removal and replacement ... can be conducted without any modifications to the structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems;

requirements.label: Accessible Public Telephones
requirements.description: In each location with public telephones, at least one must comply with accessibility requirements.
requirements.evidence: at least one telephone shall comply with Subsection 3.8.3.

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Downward changes in elevation in a barrier-free path must be signaled by tactile attention indicator surfaces per CSA B651.
requirements.evidence: a downward change in elevation shall be signaled by the use of a tactile attention indicator surface complying with Clauses 4.3.5.3.1., 4.3.5.3.3. and 4.3.5.3.4. of CSA B651

values.text: 10 dBA min above ambient, 80 dBA max

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Tactile attention indicators must be installed at the top of unenclosed stairs and at drop-off edges with elevation change >300 mm unprotected by a guard.
alternates.evidence: tactile attention indicators...shall be installed at the top of flights of stairs that are unenclosed, and at drop-off edges with a change in elevation greater than 300 mm that are unprotected by a guard.

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Visual and tactile/textural warning systems should be provided on escalator steps and floor surfaces at the top and bottom of escalators for orientation.
alternates.evidence: visual and tactile/textural warning system on escalator steps and ﬂ oor surfaces at the top and bottom of the escalator

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Tactile Walking Surface Indicators (TWSIs) must be used to alert to hazards (e.g., stairs, platform edges) and provide wayfinding for people with vision loss.
alternates.evidence: Tactile walking surface indicators (TWSIs), sometimes known as detectable warning surfaces, are standardized walking surfaces that convey information to people with vision loss through texture, and, occasionally, through sound.

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Attention TWSIs must be installed at platform edges, ferry dock edges, unprotected reflecting pools, top of stairs, both sides of ground-level railway crossings, blended curbs, and moving walkways.
alternates.evidence: CNIB recommends attention TWSIs should be used at the following locations: Platform edges... The top of stairs... Both sides of ground-level railway crossings... Blended curbs...

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Attention TWSIs on platforms/ferry docks must begin 610 mm before the drop-off, run full length of unprotected edges; at stairs, commence one tread before nosing, minimum 920 mm deep.
alternates.evidence: When attention TWSIs are used on platforms and ferry docks, CNIB recommends they begin 610 millimeters before the drop-oę , running the full length of all unprotected platform/dock edges... The depth of the TWSIs used at the top of stairs should be a minimum of 920 mm.

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: At railway crossings, attention TWSIs must be 1.8 m min to 4.6 m max from centerline of nearest rail; at blended curbs, set back 150–200 mm, min depth 610 mm.
alternates.evidence: At railway crossings, attention TWSIs should be located so that the edges of TWSIs are 1.8 m minimum and 4.6 m maximum from the centerline of the nearest rail... set back 150 mm to 200 mm from the curb’s edge, and they should extend a minimum depth of 610 mm in the direction of travel.

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Attention TWSIs must have domes 5mm ±1mm high, top diameter 12–20mm, base 10mm ±1mm wider, min 15mm between bases, spacing per table.
alternates.evidence: Attention TWSIs should have the following speciﬁ cations: 1) The height of the ﬂ at-topped domes should be 5mm +/- 1mm. 2) The diameter of the top of the ﬂ at-topped domes should be between 12 mm and 20mm. 3) The diameter of the lower base of the ﬂ at-topped domes should be 10mm +/- 1mm more than the diameter of the top. 4) The distance between the bases of adjacent domes should be a minimum of 15mm. 5) The spacing between adjacent ﬂ at-topped domes should be adjusted depending on the size of the domes, as shown in the table below.

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Guidance TWSIs must be parallel flat-topped bars, 250–550mm wide, 600mm min clearance each side, 5mm ±1mm high, top width 17–30mm, base 10mm ±1mm wider, spacing per table.
alternates.evidence: CNIB recommends guidance TWSIs have the following speciﬁ cations: They should be a minimum width of 250mm and a maximum of 550mm. They should have a minimum clearance of 600mm on either side of them. The height of the bars should be 5mm +/- 1mm. The width of the top of the ﬂ at-topped elongated bars should be between 17 mm and 30 mm. The width of the base of the bars should be 10mm +/- 1mm wider than the top.

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Guidance TWSIs crossing the path of travel (e.g., bus shelters) must be at least 550mm wide.
alternates.evidence: When used to cross the path of travel, in locations such as bus shelters, guidance TWSIs should be a minimum width of 550mm to ensure detection.

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Tactile warning strips in contrasting colours must be used at the top of escalators, ramps, moving walkways, stairways, and platform edges not served by handrails or guards.
alternates.evidence: tactile warning strips in contrasting colours shall be used at the top of escalators, ramps, moving walkways, stairways, and at the edge of platforms not served by handrails or guards

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Use tactile cues (different flooring), colour contrast, and changes in illumination to warn of changes in direction or use.
alternates.evidence: tactile cues, i.e., dię erent types of ﬂ ooring that mark a pathway, or change from one area to another, thus serving as a warning that a change in direction is to take place

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Apply tactile warning surfaces and auditory cues to help users map their environment and identify hazards.
alternates.evidence: auditory cues...tactile and kinaesthetic cues, such as the application of dięerent ﬂoor surfaces and approved tactile warning surfaces

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Provide warnings of hazards and errors.
alternates.evidence: Provide warnings of hazards and errors.

alternates.label: Warning Indicators
alternates.description: Visible indicators must extinguish when the car arrives at the designated floor; audible signals must be 10 dBA above ambient, not exceeding 80 dBA.
alternates.evidence: The visible indication shall extinguish when the car arrives at the designated ﬂoor. The audible signal shall be 10 dBA minimum above ambient, but shall not exceed 80 dBA maximum.

values.text: 10 dBA min above ambient, 80 dBA max

requirements.label: Tactile Attention Indicator Configuration
requirements.description: Tactile attention indicator surfaces must use truncated domes 4–5 mm high, top diameter 12–25 mm, base diameter 10±1 mm greater, in a square grid, with specified spacing.
requirements.evidence: A tactile attention indicator surface shall be composed of truncated domes: a. with a height of between 4 and 5 mm; b. with the top diameter between 12 and 25 mm and the base diameter 10 ± 1 mm greater than the top diameter; c. arranged in a square grid; and d. with a centre-to-centre distance of adjacent domes complying with Table 5

requirements.label: Tactile Attention Indicator Installation
requirements.description: Tactile attention indicator surfaces must be installed along the full width of the hazard, 600–650 mm deep, with one side against the edge of the hazard.
requirements.evidence: it shall be installed along the full width of the hazard: a. to a depth between 600 and 650 mm; and b. with one side against the edge of the hazard

requirements.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
requirements.description: Tactile attention indicator surfaces must have a luminance (colour) contrast of at least 50% with adjacent surfaces using the Michelson contrast formula.
requirements.evidence: it shall have a luminance (colour) contrast of at least 50% with the adjacent surface using the Michelson contrast formula

alternates.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
alternates.description: Door or door frame leading from public corridors to public areas or exits must have readily apparent visual contrast with adjacent wall surfaces.
alternates.evidence: doorways ... shall be provided with a door or door frame that has a readily apparent visual contrast with adjacent wall surfaces.

alternates.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
alternates.description: Doorways must be identified by contrasting colours for door frames, handles, awnings, etc.
alternates.evidence: Doorways shall be identified by the use of contrasting colours for door frames, handles, awnings, etc.

alternates.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
alternates.description: TWSIs should be colour-contrasted with the surrounding walking surface; industrial yellow is preferred, or light on dark/dark on light.
alternates.evidence: TWSIs should be colour-contrasted with the surrounding walking surface. Industrial yellow is the preferred colour. However, a light colour on a dark ground surface or a dark colour on a light ground surface also works eę ectively.

alternates.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
alternates.description: Where a maze entrance exists for a public washroom, the floor finish shall be high in colour contrast to the wall finish for wayfinding.
alternates.evidence: the ﬂ oor ﬁ nish shall be slip-resistant and be high in colour contrast to the wall ﬁ nish. This is to provide safe and appropriate wayﬁ nding for people who have visual disabilities

alternates.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
alternates.description: Signs and floor identification for orientation should offer maximum colour contrast, preferably white on black or black on white, and be glare-free.
alternates.evidence: it is recommended that white on black or black on white is used, as this combination produces the best legibility...sign surfaces be processed to prevent glare.

alternates.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
alternates.description: Use colour/contrast cues to define building landmarks, identify obstacles, and assist with locating building controls.
alternates.evidence: colour/contrast cues, which can be used to deﬁne building landmarks, help one identify obstacles in a path of travel, and assist with locating building controls

alternates.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
alternates.description: Provide adequate contrast between essential information and its surroundings.
alternates.evidence: Provide adequate contrast between essential information and its surroundings.

requirements.label: Tactile Direction Indicator Configuration
requirements.description: Tactile direction indicator surfaces must use flat-topped, parallel, elongated bars 4–5 mm high, top width 17–30 mm, base width top+10±1 mm, top length ≥270 mm.
requirements.evidence: A tactile direction indicator surface shall be composed of flat- topped, parallel, elongated bars having: a. a height of 4 to 5 mm; b. a top width between 17 and 30 mm and a base width 10 ± 1 mm greater than the top width; ... d. a top length not less than 270 mm

requirements.label: Overhead Obstruction Clearance
requirements.description: Overhead objects along walkways must be at least 1980 mm high to avoid becoming a hazard for persons with visual impairments.
requirements.evidence: Overhead objects (e.g., signage, tree branches/limbs, free-standing staircases/escalators) should be at a height or 1 980 mm or greater to avoid becoming a hazard.

requirements.label: Passenger Loading Zone Accessibility
requirements.description: Exterior passenger-loading zones must have an access aisle at least 1500 mm wide and 6000 mm long, a curb ramp if needed, and a clear height of at least 2750 mm.
requirements.evidence: If an exterior passenger-loading zone is provided, it shall have a. an access aisle not less than 1 500 mm wide and 6 000 mm long... c. a clear height of not less than 2 750 mm...

requirements.label: Rest Area Widening
requirements.description: At intervals not exceeding every 9 m, walkways should be widened to a minimum of 2000 mm for a length of 2000 mm to act as a rest area.
requirements.evidence: it is recommended that at intervals not exceeding every 9 m, the walkway should be widened to a minimum of 2 000 mm for a length of 2 000 mm to act as a rest area

requirements.label: Edge Protection for Ramps
requirements.description: Ramps and landings not at grade or adjacent to a wall must have edge protection: a curb at least 75 mm high or a raised barrier/rail not more than 100 mm from the surface.
requirements.evidence: Ramps and landings not at grade or adjacent to a wall shall have edge protection consisting of a. a curb not less than 75 mm high, or b. a raised barrier or rail located not more than 100 mm from the ramp or landing surface.

requirements.label: Grating Openings in Walkways
requirements.description: Gratings in walkways must have a maximum clear opening of 13 mm.
requirements.evidence: If these covers must be in walkways, the gratings shall have a maximum clear opening of 13 mm.

requirements.label: Tactile Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Tactile warning indicators are required for ease of cane detection and safety at curb ramps and other key locations.
requirements.evidence: Reminder: Tactile warning indicators are required for ease of cane detection and safety.

requirements.label: Level Area at Entrances
requirements.description: A level area of at least 1500 mm by 1500 mm is to be provided in front of the entrance to allow ease of access into or out of a building.
requirements.evidence: A level area of at least 1 500 mm by 1 500 mm is to be provided in front of the entrance to allow ease of access into or out of a building.

requirements.label: Obstruction-Free Area
requirements.description: Areas free from obstructions (e.g., directional signs, tree branches, guy wires) are required to aid persons with visual impairments and mobility aid users.
requirements.evidence: The requirement to have an area free from obstructions is primarily to aid persons with visual impairments and persons who are blind or elderly who may require the use of a mobility aid.

requirements.label: Colour Contrast on Ramps and Landings
requirements.description: Ramps, landings, and treads accessible to the public must have either a colour contrast or a distinctive pattern to demarcate the leading edge and the beginning/end of a ramp.
requirements.evidence: shall have either a colour contrast or a distinctive pattern to demarcate the leading edge of the tread and the leading edge of the landing, as well as the beginning and end of a ramp.

requirements.label: Ramp Edge Protection and Visual Contrast
requirements.description: Ramps should have curbs, handrails, and edge protection with contrasting colours for safety.
requirements.evidence: Curbs can be of contrasting colour from the ramp and handrails can be a contrasting colour from the adjacent surface as best practice for edge protection at the sides of ramps.

requirements.label: Threshold Height and Bevel
requirements.description: Door thresholds in barrier-free paths must not exceed 13 mm in height and must be beveled for wheelchair passage.
requirements.evidence: A threshold for a doorway referred to in Sentence (2) and (3) shall be not more than 13 mm higher than the finished floor surface and shall be beveled to facilitate the passage of wheelchairs.

values.text: threshold not more than 13 mm high

alternates.label: Threshold Height and Bevel
alternates.description: A threshold for a doorway must not be more than 13 mm higher than the finished floor and must be bevelled for wheelchair passage.
alternates.evidence: A threshold for a doorway... shall be not more than 13 mm higher than the ﬁ nished ﬂ oor surface and shall be bevelled to facilitate the passage of wheelchairs.

requirements.label: Cane-Detectable Guard for Power Doors
requirements.description: Install cane-detectable guards on the hinged side of power-assisted doors that swing into the path of travel.
requirements.evidence: A cane-detectable guard shall be installed on the hinged side of power-assisted doors that swing open into the path of travel.

requirements.label: Door Opening Force
requirements.description: Doors in barrier-free paths must open with a force not exceeding 38 N (exterior) or 22 N (interior/sliding).
requirements.evidence: a door in a barrier-free path of travel shall open when the force applied ... is not more than 38 N in the case of an exterior swinging door, 22 N in the case of an interior swinging door, or 22 N in the case of a sliding door.

alternates.label: Door Opening Force
alternates.description: A closer for a door in a barrier-free path of travel must permit opening with a force not more than 38 N for exterior doors.
alternates.evidence: a closer for a door in a barrier-free path of travel shall be designed to permit the door to open when the force applied... is not more than a) 38 N in the case of an exterior door

requirements.label: Door Closer Timing
requirements.description: Interior doors in barrier-free paths must have a closing period of at least 3 seconds from 70° open to 75 mm from closed.
requirements.evidence: a closer for an interior door in a barrier-free path of travel shall have a closing period of not less than 3 s measured from when the door is in an open position of 70° to the doorway, to when the door reaches a point 75 mm from the closed position

values.text: closing period not less than 3 s

requirements.label: Clear Space Beside Doors
requirements.description: Swinging doors in barrier-free paths must have clear space on the latch side: 600 mm (toward approach), 300 mm (away from approach).
requirements.evidence: a swinging door in a barrier-free path of travel shall have a clear space on the latch side ... not less than 600 mm beyond the edge of the door opening if the door swings toward the approach side, and 300 mm ... if the door swings away

requirements.label: Vestibule Space for Wheelchairs
requirements.description: Vestibules in barrier-free paths must allow wheelchair movement and provide at least 1,350 mm plus door width between doors in series.
requirements.evidence: a vestibule located in a barrier-free path of travel shall ... provide a distance between 2 doors in series of not less than 1 350 mm plus the width of any door that swings into the space

requirements.label: Clear Floor Space at Doors (Pull Side)
requirements.description: Clear floor space on the pull side of swinging doors in barrier-free paths must be at least 1,700 mm by 1,500 mm.
requirements.evidence: the clear floor space on the pull side of a swinging door in a barrier-free path of travel shall be level within a rectangular area of not less than 1 700 mm by 1 500 mm

requirements.label: Clear Floor Space at Doors (Push/Sliding Side)
requirements.description: Clear floor space on the push side of swinging doors and both sides of sliding doors in barrier-free paths must be at least 1,200 mm by 1,500 mm.
requirements.evidence: the clear floor space on the push side of a swinging door and on each side of a sliding door in a barrier-free path of travel shall be level within a rectangular area ... not less than 1 200 mm ... not less than 1 500 mm

requirements.label: Reduced Clear Floor Space for Power Doors
requirements.description: Where equipped with a power door operator, clear floor space parallel to the closed door may be reduced to 1,000 mm.
requirements.evidence: where a door ... is equipped with a power door operator ... the width of the clear floor space parallel to the closed door is permitted to be reduced to not less than 1 000 mm.

requirements.label: Accessible Door Handles
requirements.description: Lever handles with ends turned toward the door are recommended over knob-type handles for ease of use and safety for people with limited strength or visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: Knob-type handles are not recommended... Lever handles with the ends turned toward the door are less of a hazard

requirements.label: Clear Floor Space at Doors

alternates.evidence: control buttons ... shall be identiﬁed with tactile and visual symbols as shown in Table 2.26.12.1.

requirements.description: Accessible tables must be located on accessible paths, with firm, stable surfaces no steeper than 1:50 (2%).
requirements.evidence: locate on an accessible path of travel or trail; ensure ground surface leading to and under tables is firm, stable, and no steeper than 1:50 (2%)

requirements.label: Accessible Table Signage
requirements.description: Provide directional signage at strategic locations to identify accessible tables and/or public use eating areas.
requirements.evidence: provide directional signage at strategic locations to identify the location(s) of accessible tables and or public use eating areas;

requirements.label: Accessible Barbecue Placement
requirements.description: Barbecues in outdoor public-use eating areas must be placed away from accessible paths and on surfaces with high tonal and textural contrast.
requirements.evidence: ensure that they are placed away from the accessible path of travel and on a surface with high tonal and textural contrast with the adjacent surfaces;

requirements.label: Accessible Washroom Features in Outdoor Areas
requirements.description: Where washrooms are provided in outdoor public use eating areas, ensure at least one universal toilet room per cluster of regular washrooms.
requirements.evidence: where washrooms are provided, ensure accessible features (e.g., at least one universal toilet room per cluster of regular washrooms).

requirements.label: Trailhead Information Signage
requirements.description: Integrate designated trailhead information signage at key entrance/exit points, intermediate areas on lengthy trails, or decision points.
requirements.evidence: Ensure designated trailhead information signage are integrated as part of the trail design at key entrance and exit points along the trail, intermediate areas on lengthy trails, or decision points...

requirements.label: Trail Entrance Clear Opening
requirements.description: Trail entrances must provide 850 mm to 1000 mm clear opening, and be maintained clear of obstructions.
requirements.evidence: provide 850 mm to 1000 mm clear opening whether entrance includes a gate, bollard, or other entrance design; and ensure that entrances are maintained and clear of obstructions...

requirements.label: Trail Clear Width and Passing Spaces
requirements.description: Provide trail clear width of 1000 mm (minimum) to 1800 mm (preferred); where less than 1800 mm, provide 1800 mm x 1800 mm passing spaces at intervals ≤30 m.
requirements.evidence: provide clear width of 1000 mm (minimum) to 1800 mm (preferred); where the clear width is less than 1800 mm, provide a passing space of 1800 mm wide by 1800 mm (minimum) long, at intervals no more than 30 m

values.text: 1000 mm min width, 1800 mm preferred; passing space 1800x1800 mm every 30 m

alternates.label: Trail Clear Width and Passing Spaces
alternates.description: Trails must have a clear width of 1000 mm minimum (1800 mm preferred); if less than 1800 mm, provide 1800 mm x 1800 mm passing spaces every 30 m.
alternates.evidence: provide clear width of 1000 mm (minimum) to 1800 mm (preferred); where the clear width is less than 1800 mm, provide a passing space of 1800 mm wide by 1800 mm (minimum) long, at intervals no more than 30 m

values.text: 1000 mm min width, 1800 mm preferred; passing space 1800x1800 mm every 30 m

requirements.label: Trail Headroom Clearance
requirements.description: Ensure headroom clearance is 2100 mm minimum above the trail and no obstructions or projections along trail.
requirements.evidence: ensure headroom clearance is 2100 mm (minimum) above the trail; and ensure that there are no obstructions or projections along trail.

alternates.label: Trail Headroom Clearance
alternates.description: Ensure headroom clearance is 2100 mm (minimum) above the trail.
alternates.evidence: ensure headroom clearance is 2100 mm (minimum) above the trail;

alternates.label: Trail Headroom Clearance
alternates.description: Headroom clearance above the trail must be at least 2100 mm.
alternates.evidence: Is the HEADROOM CLEARANCE above the trail at least 2100 mm?

requirements.label: Trail Surface Requirements
requirements.description: Trail surfaces must be firm, stable, resistant to weather, and have openings no greater than 20 mm (13 mm preferred), oriented perpendicular to travel.
requirements.evidence: ensure that surface is firm and stable;...openings must not allow passage of an object that has a diameter of more than 20 mm (13 mm diameter preferred), and that any elongated openings are oriented approximately perpendicular to the direction of travel;

requirements.label: Trail Surface Information Signage
requirements.description: Type of surface and expected changing conditions must be identified in information signage at trailhead.
requirements.evidence: ensure that type of surface used and expected conditions that may change over time are identiﬁ ed in information signage provided at trailhead.

alternates.label: Trail Surface Information Signage
alternates.description: Type of surface used and expected conditions that may change over time must be identified in information signage at trailhead.
alternates.evidence: ensure that type of surface used and expected conditions that may change over time are identified in information signage provided at trailhead.

requirements.label: Edge Protection for Trails and Boardwalks
requirements.description: Provide edge protection at least 50 mm high along recreational trails and boardwalks adjacent to water or drop-offs, designed to allow drainage.
requirements.evidence: provide edge protection with the following requirements: ... top of the edge protection at 50 mm (minimum) high above the trail surface

requirements.label: Boardwalk Passing Spaces
requirements.description: Where boardwalk clear width is less than 1800 mm, provide passing spaces 1800 mm x 1800 mm at intervals no more than 30 m.
requirements.evidence: where the clear width is less than 1800 mm, provide a passing space of 1800 mm wide by 1800 mm (minimum) long, at intervals no more than 30 m

values.text: Passing space: 1800 mm x 1800 mm every 30 m

requirements.label: Trail Surface Openings
requirements.description: Trail surface openings must not allow passage of objects >20 mm diameter (13 mm preferred); elongated openings oriented perpendicular to travel.
requirements.evidence: openings must not allow passage of an object that has a diameter of more than 20 mm (13 mm diameter preferred), and that any elongated openings are oriented approximately perpendicular to the direction of travel;

values.text: Trail: 13 mm max; Boardwalk: 20 mm max (13 mm preferred)

alternates.label: Trail Surface Openings
alternates.description: Ensure trail and boardwalk surfaces are firm, stable, and openings do not allow passage of objects >13 mm (trail) or >20 mm (boardwalk), elongated openings perpendicular to travel.
alternates.evidence: ensure that openings must not allow passage of an object that has a diameter greater than 13 mm ... boardwalk ... more than 20 mm (13 mm diameter preferred)

values.text: Trail: 13 mm max; Boardwalk: 20 mm max (13 mm preferred)

requirements.label: Beach Access Route Entrances
requirements.description: Provide 1000 mm clear opening at entrances, including gates or bollards, for beach access routes.
requirements.evidence: provide 1000 mm clear opening whether entrance includes a gate, bollard. or other entrance design.

requirements.label: Signage Contrast and Font
requirements.description: Ensure signage text has high tonal contrast with its background and uses sans serif font for visual recognition.
requirements.evidence: ensure that signage text has high tonal contrast with its background ... characters that use a sans serif font.

requirements.label: Trail Information Consistency
requirements.description: Where other media (websites, brochures) provide trail information, include the same details as trailhead signage.
requirements.evidence: provide the same information identified on the trailhead signage

requirements.label: Accessible Play Surface Materials
requirements.description: Play spaces must provide accessible surface materials such as poured-in-place rubber, accessible turf, rubber mats and tiles, bonded and engineered wood fibers, or shredded rubber.
requirements.evidence: provide accessible surface materials for play spaces such as poured-in-place rubber, accessible turf, rubber mats and tiles, bonded and engineered wood ﬁ bers or shredded rubber.

alternates.label: Accessible Play Surface Materials
alternates.description: Provide accessible surface materials for play spaces such as poured-in-place rubber, accessible turf, rubber mats and tiles, bonded and engineered wood fibers or shredded rubber.
alternates.evidence: Provide accessible surface materials for play spaces such as poured-in-place rubber, accessible turf, rubber mats and tiles, bonded and engineered wood fibers or shredded rubber.

requirements.label: Elevated Play Component Access
requirements.description: At least 50% of elevated play components must be connected to a ramp or transfer system. For 20 or more components: 25% ramp, 25% ramp or transfer system; for less than 20: transfer system permitted.
requirements.evidence: Ensure that at least 50% of elevated play components are connected to a ramp or transfer system, as identified in Table 13 below.

requirements.label: Transfer System Dimensions
requirements.description: Provide transfer systems to connect elevated or ground-level play components. Minimum clear floor space of 915 mm wide by 1370 mm long adjacent to all transfer locations.
requirements.evidence: provide a minimum clear floor space of 915 mm wide by 1370 mm long adjacent to all transfer locations onto play components

values.text: 915 mm x 1370 mm clear floor space

requirements.label: Turning Space for Mobility Aids
requirements.description: Provide clear turning space for mobility aids of 1675 mm (preferred) or 1500 mm (minimum) diameter on the same level as play components.
requirements.evidence: provide clear turning space for mobility aids of 1675 mm (preferred) or 1500 mm (minimum) diameter on the same level as play components

requirements.label: Ground-Level Play Component Ratios
requirements.description: Provide the minimum number and variety of ground-level play components along accessible routes, as determined by the number of elevated play components (see Table 14).
requirements.evidence: Provide the ratio of ground-level play component alternatives, compared to elevated play components, as identiﬁ ed in Table 14.

values.text: See Table 14 for ratios (e.g., 2-4 elevated: 1 ground-level, 1 type)

alternates.label: Ground-Level Play Component Ratios
alternates.description: Provide the minimum number and variety of ground-level play components along an accessible route, as per CAN / CSA Z614-14 (Annex H) Table 14.
alternates.evidence: Provide the ratio of ground-level play component alternatives, compared to elevated play components, as identified in Table 14 below.

values.text: See Table 14 for ratios (e.g., 2-4 elevated: 1 ground-level, 1 type)

requirements.label: Walking Surface Openings
requirements.description: Walking surfaces in barrier-free paths must have no opening permitting passage of a sphere more than 13 mm in diameter; elongated openings must be perpendicular to travel.
requirements.evidence: have no opening that will permit the passage of a sphere more than 13 mm in diam.

requirements.label: Bevelled Changes in Level
requirements.description: Changes in level not more than 13 mm must be bevelled at a maximum slope of 1 in 2.
requirements.evidence: be bevelled at a maximum slope of 1 in 2 at changes in level not more than 13 mm

values.text: max slope 1:2 for ≤13 mm level change

requirements.label: Escalator Step Demarcation
requirements.description: Escalator steps must have yellow demarcation lines: back strip 38–50 mm wide; side lines at least 13 mm, not exceeding 50 mm.
requirements.evidence: demarcation lines on the step tread...yellow strip with a minimum of 38 mm and a maximum of 50 mm in width...side lines...at least 13 mm wide and shall not exceed 50 mm

requirements.label: Visitable Housing Features
requirements.description: Visitable homes must have no-step entrance, doorways and hallways min 900 mm wide, and wheelchair accessible bathroom with 1500 mm turning diameter and grab bars.
requirements.evidence: No-step entrance...Wider doorways (min 900 mm) and hallways (900–1200 mm)...Wheelchair accessible bathroom...min 1500 mm turning diameter.

values.text: door/hall min 900 mm; bathroom turning diameter 1500 mm

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Stall Dimensions
requirements.description: A parking stall for persons using a wheelchair or mobility aid must be 2.4 m wide, adjacent to a 2.4 m wide access aisle, with demarcation indicating no parking.
requirements.evidence: be designed as a 2.4 m wide parking stall adjacent to a 2.4 m wide access aisle where the access aisle is demarcated to indicate no parking

values.text: 2.4 m wide stall + 2.4 m wide access aisle

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Signage
requirements.description: Accessible parking stalls must be clearly marked with a vertically mounted sign (1,600–2,500 mm from finished surface) and the International Symbol of Access painted on the pavement.
requirements.evidence: clearly marked and identiﬁ ed by i) a vertically mounted sign...with the centre of the sign between 1 600 to 2 500 mm from the ﬁ nished surface, and ii) the International Symbol of Access painted on the pavement

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Height Clearance
requirements.description: Accessible parking level should allow a minimum 3,000-mm height clearance in a parkade to accommodate larger vehicles used by drivers or passengers with a disability.
requirements.evidence: The accessible parking level should allow a minimum 3 000-mm height clearance in a parkade in order to accommodate larger vehicles...

requirements.label: Passenger Loading Zone Dimensions
requirements.description: Exterior passenger loading zones must have an access aisle at least 1,500 mm wide and 6,000 mm long, a curb ramp if needed, and a clear height of at least 2,750 mm.
requirements.evidence: an access aisle not less than 1 500 mm wide and 6 000 mm long...a clear height of not less than 2 750 mm at the pull-up space

values.text: 1,500 mm x 6,000 mm aisle, 2,750 mm height

requirements.label: Wheel Stops for Sidewalk Protection
requirements.description: Wheel stops should be used to prevent vehicles from intruding onto the walk or path, maintaining a barrier-free path of travel on pedestrian walkways.
requirements.evidence: wheel stops should be used to prevent vehicles from intruding onto the walk or path.

requirements.label: Curb Ramp Placement
requirements.description: Curb ramps shall NOT be located in front of designated stall; they should be between stalls (access aisle) or to the side/adjacent to the stall.
requirements.evidence: Curb ramps shall NOT be located in front of designated stall because the vehicle will block access...In a vehicle pull-up space, the curb ramp shall be located between stalls (such as the access aisle) or to the side of or adjacent to the designated stall.

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Stall Markings
requirements.description: Only the accessibility symbol in yellow needs to be located on the pavement of the stall to identify it as accessible; painting the entire area blue is not required.
requirements.evidence: Only the symbol in yellow needs to be located on the pavement of the stall in order to identify it as an accessible parking stall.

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Directional Signage
requirements.description: Where the location of designated parking spaces is not obvious or is distant, directional signs should be placed along the route leading to them.
requirements.evidence: directional signs should be placed along the route leading to them.

requirements.label: Ramp Edge Protection
requirements.description: Ramps must have provisions such as curbs, handrails, or guards to prevent wheelchairs or mobility aids from going over the edge.
requirements.evidence: A variety of provisions can be used to prevent people using wheelchairs or other mobility aids from accidentally going over the edge of a ramp. Curbs are often combined with handrails and guards.

requirements.label: Accessible Door Hardware
requirements.description: Door operating devices must not require tight grasping or twisting of the wrist; lever handles are preferred.
requirements.evidence: Door operating devices shall be of a design which does not require tight grasping and twisting of the wrist as the only means of operation.

requirements.label: Door Hardware Height
requirements.description: Door hardware should be installed between 900 mm and 1,065 mm above the finished floor.
requirements.evidence: Door hardware should be installed between 900 mm and 1 065 mm above the ﬁ nished ﬂ oor.

requirements.label: Door Latch Side Clearance
requirements.description: Doorways must provide at least 600 mm of clear space on the latch side for wheelchair or mobility aid users.
requirements.evidence: the doorway shall provide a minimum of 600 mm of clear space on the latch side of the door to allow a person in a wheelchair, or those using other mobility aids, ease of movement and safety.

requirements.label: Threshold Visual Contrast
requirements.description: Thresholds should be identified by an alternate colour or brightness to indicate a change in level.
requirements.evidence: the threshold should be identiﬁ ed by an alternate colour or brightness to indicate a change in level from area to area.

requirements.label: Area Rug Secured
requirements.description: Shower stalls must have a bevelled threshold not more than 13 mm higher than the finished floor.
requirements.evidence: have a bevelled threshold not more than 13 mm higher than the ﬁ nished ﬂ oor

values.text: ≤100 mm projection unless <680 mm clearance below

alternates.label: Area Rug Secured
alternates.description: Any shelf or projection above a lavatory shall be located so that it will not be a hazard.
alternates.evidence: Any shelf or projection above a lavatory shall be located so that it will not be a hazard.

alternates.label: Area Rug Secured
alternates.description: Obstructions within 1,980 mm of the floor must not project more than 100 mm into corridors unless clearance below is less than 680 mm.
alternates.evidence: obstructions located within 1 980 mm of the ﬂ oor shall not project more than 100 mm horizontally into an exit passageway...

values.text: ≤100 mm projection unless <680 mm clearance below

alternates.label: Area Rug Secured
alternates.description: Stair nosing must be compatible to accept a horizontal strip that is slip resistant and extends the full width of the tread.
alternates.evidence: be compatible, without the need to modify the stair structure, treads, risers, or coverings, to accept a horizontal strip 50 ± 10 mm deep that: ... is slip resistant; ... extends the full width of the tread.

requirements.label: Elevator Car Stretcher Accommodation
requirements.description: At least one elevator must have inside dimensions to accommodate a patient stretcher 2,010 mm long and 610 mm wide in the prone position.
requirements.evidence: all storeys shall be served by at least one elevator which has inside dimensions that will accommodate and provide adequate access for a patient stretcher 2 010 mm long and 610 mm wide in the prone position.

requirements.label: Elevator Identification
requirements.description: An elevator satisfying stretcher accommodation requirements shall be clearly identified on the main entrance level of the building.
requirements.evidence: An elevator satisfying the requirements of Sentence (1) shall be clearly identiﬁ ed on the main entrance level of the building.

requirements.label: Platform Lift Travel Limits
requirements.description: The travel of a vertical platform lift shall not exceed 2,500 mm for unenclosed type or 7,000 mm for enclosed type.
requirements.evidence: The travel of a vertical platform lift shall not exceed the following (a)  2500 mm for unenclosed type; or (b)  7000 mm for enclosed type

requirements.label: Elevator Minimum Car Size for Lift Substitution
requirements.description: When an elevator is used instead of a lift, the car must meet the minimum size in Table 1 of CSA-B355 and is restricted to a maximum rise of 7,000 mm.
requirements.evidence: the car cannot be less than the minimum as set out in Table 1 of CSA-B355, and the elevator is restricted to a maximum rise of 7000 mm.

requirements.label: Accessible Drinking Fountains
requirements.description: At least one drinking fountain must be barrier-free, with a spout no more than 915 mm above the floor and controls operable with one hand using ≤22 N force or automatic.
requirements.evidence: If drinking fountains are provided, at least one shall be barrier-free and shall a) have a spout located near the front of the unit not more than 915 mm above the floor, and b) be equipped with controls...using one hand with a force of not more than 22 N, or be automatically operable.

requirements.label: Drinking Fountain Cane Detectability
requirements.description: Drinking fountains on barrier-free paths should have cane-detectable barriers on each side, with enough space for wheelchairs or scooters.
requirements.evidence: when the fountain is located in a barrier-free path of travel, a partition or cane-detectable barrier on each side of the wall-mounted drinking fountain should be provided. Enough space should still be provided between the partitions for persons who use wheelchairs or scooters.

requirements.label: Obstacle Recessing on Barrier-Free Paths
requirements.description: Telephone kiosks, fire extinguishers, vending machines, planters, and ashtrays should be recessed or located alongside barrier-free paths to avoid being obstacles.
requirements.evidence: Telephone kiosks, fire extinguishers, and vending machines should be recessed on a barrier-free path of travel to prevent them from being obstacles...Planters and ashtrays should be recessed or located alongside the designated barrier-free path of travel.

requirements.label: Signage and Fixture Height for Visual Safety
requirements.description: Bottom edges of signs and bases of wall lighting fixtures should be at least 2 m above the finished floor to prevent hazards for people with visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: The bottom edges of signs and the bases of wall lighting fixtures should be located no lower than 2 m, measured from the finished floor surface, to prevent them from being hazards to persons with visual disabilities.

requirements.label: Accessible Building Controls
requirements.description: Controls for building services must be accessible to wheelchair users, operable with one hand, and mounted between 400 mm and 1,200 mm above the floor.
requirements.evidence: controls...shall be accessible to a person in a wheelchair, operable with one hand and mounted between 400 mm and 1 200 mm above the floor.

requirements.label: Braille and Colour Contrast for Controls
requirements.description: Raised lettering and/or Braille and colour contrast should be used for building service controls to aid persons with visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: Raised lettering and/or Braille should be added to help persons with visual disabilities to access building service controls...using colour contrast to the background for ease of identification

requirements.label: Light Switch Height
requirements.description: Light switches should be located between 1,100 mm and 1,200 mm above the finished floor for improved access.
requirements.evidence: Light switches should be located between 1 100 mm and 1 200 mm above the finished floor to improve access for people in wheelchairs

alternates.label: Light Switch Height
alternates.description: Light switches should be mounted between 1200 mm and 1400 mm from the finished floor (measured to the centre line of the plate).
alternates.evidence: Light switches should be mounted between 1 200 mm – 1 400 mm when measured from the centre line of the plate to the finished floor.

requirements.label: Electrical Outlet Height
requirements.description: Electrical outlets should be at least 650 mm from the finished floor to the center of the plate.
requirements.evidence: Electrical outlets should be located at a minimum of 650 mm when measured from the centre of the plate to the finished floor

alternates.label: Electrical Outlet Height
alternates.description: Electrical outlets should be no lower than 600 mm from the finished floor (measured to the centre line of the plate).
alternates.evidence: Electrical outlets should be no lower than 600 mm when measured from the centre line of the plate to the finished floor.

requirements.label: Barrier-Free Public Washrooms
requirements.description: All public washrooms on a barrier-free path must be barrier-free, except in certain residential or small suite cases with accessible alternatives within 45 m.
requirements.evidence: all washrooms in a barrier-free path of travel shall be barrier-free...A washroom need not conform...if...there are barrier-free washrooms on the same floor area within 45 m.

requirements.label: Barrier-Free Water Closet on Entrance Storey
requirements.description: At least one barrier-free water closet must be provided on the entrance storey unless a barrier-free path leads to accessible water closets elsewhere.
requirements.evidence: at least one barrier-free water closet shall be provided in the entrance storey, unless a) a barrier-free path of travel is provided to barrier-free water closets elsewhere in the building

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room in Transportation Terminals
requirements.description: At least one universal toilet room must be provided in a regional transportation terminal.
requirements.evidence: at least one universal toilet room conforming to Article 3.8.3.12. shall be provided in a regional transportation terminal.

requirements.label: Barrier-Free Stall Ratio in Washrooms
requirements.description: A barrier-free stall must be provided for every 10 stalls or part thereof in public washrooms and for temporary uses like fairs and festivals.
requirements.evidence: a barrier-free stall shall be provided for every 10 stalls or part thereof...For temporary uses, such as outdoor fairs and festivals, a barrier-free stall shall be provided for every 10 stalls or part thereof.

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Minimum Size
requirements.description: Universal toilet rooms must have no internal dimension between walls less than 1,700 mm and should be at least 2,100 mm x 1,700 mm.
requirements.evidence: have no internal dimension between the walls that is less than 1 700 mm

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Turning Space
requirements.description: Universal toilet rooms must permit a wheelchair to turn in an open space not less than 1,500 mm in diameter.
requirements.evidence: be designed to permit a wheelchair to turn in an open space not less than 1 500 mm in diameter.

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Door Hardware
requirements.description: Universal toilet room doors must have a latch-operating mechanism operable with a closed fist, located 900–1,000 mm above the floor.
requirements.evidence: a latch-operating mechanism that is operable with a closed ﬁ st, located not less than 900 mm and not more than 1 000 mm above the ﬂ oor

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Grab Bars
requirements.description: Universal toilet rooms must have grab bars conforming to specified dimensions, including L-shaped and horizontal bars.
requirements.evidence: The horizontal grab bar located adjacent to the water closet shall be installed 500 mm from the wall to the midpoint of the grab bar.

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Coat Hook
requirements.description: A coat hook should be located not less than 1,100 mm from the floor, measured to the centre line of the hook.
requirements.evidence: A coat hook ... should be located not less than 1 100 mm from the ﬂ oor, and measured to the centre line of the hook

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Toilet Height
requirements.description: The toilet shall be no lower than 430 mm ± 30 mm from the floor to the surface of the toilet seat.
requirements.evidence: The toilet shall be no lower than 430 mm ± 30 mm when measured from the ﬂ oor to the surface of the toilet seat.

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Emergency Release
requirements.description: Universal toilet room doors must be capable of being locked from the inside and released from the outside in case of emergency.
requirements.evidence: have a door capable of being locked from the inside and released from the outside in case of emergency

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Door Pull
requirements.description: If the door swings outward, a door pull not less than 140 mm long must be located on the inside, 200–300 mm from the hinged side, 900–1,000 mm above the floor.
requirements.evidence: a door pull not less than 140 mm long located on the inside so that its midpoint is not less than 200 mm and not more than 300 mm from the hinged side of the door and not less than 900 mm and not more than 1 000 mm above the ﬂ oor

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Automatic Door Opener
requirements.description: It is recommended that automatic door openers on both the interior and exterior of the washroom be installed for ease of entry and egress.
requirements.evidence: It is recommended that automatic door openers on both the interior and exterior of the washroom be installed for ease of entry and egress.

requirements.label: Lavatory Pipe Insulation
requirements.description: Hot-water and drain pipes under lavatories must be insulated to prevent burn hazards to wheelchair users.
requirements.evidence: Hot-water and drain pipes under lavatories shall be insulated to prevent the pipes from becoming a burn hazard to persons who use wheelchairs.

requirements.label: Lavatory Faucet Operation
requirements.description: Lavatories must be equipped with faucets that operate automatically or have lever-type handles that do not close under spring action.
requirements.evidence: Lavatories required by Sentence (1) shall be equipped with faucets that a) operate automatically, or b) have lever-type handles that do not close under spring action.

requirements.label: Lavatory Soap and Towel Dispenser Height
requirements.description: Soap and towel dispensers must be located close to the lavatory, not more than 1,200 mm above the floor, and accessible to persons in wheelchairs.
requirements.evidence: soap dispenser located close to the lavatory, not more than 1 200 mm above the ﬂ oor and accessible to persons in wheelchairs

requirements.label: Mirror Accessibility
requirements.description: If mirrors are provided, at least one must be mounted with its bottom edge not more than 1,000 mm above the floor or be inclined to be usable by a person in a wheelchair.
requirements.evidence: at least one mirror shall be a) mounted with its bottom edge not more than 1 000 mm above the ﬂ oor, or b) be inclined to the vertical to be usable by a person in a wheelchair.

requirements.label: Mirror Mounting Above Lavatory
requirements.description: Tilt mirrors should be mounted no more than 200 mm above the lavatory when measured from the surface of the sink or counter.
requirements.evidence: The mirror should be mounted no more than 200 mm above the lavatory when measured from the surface of the sink or counter.

requirements.label: Lavatory Clearance for Wheelchair
requirements.description: Lavatory should have a clearance of 870 mm from the lowest part of the apron to the floor to allow a person in a wheelchair to maneuver beneath.
requirements.evidence: It is preferred that the lavatory should have a clearance of 870 mm measured from the lowest part of the apron to the ﬂ oor

requirements.label: Lavatory Rim Height in Universal Toilet Room
requirements.description: The rim height of the sink or top of the counter may not be greater than 875 mm from the surface to the finished floor.
requirements.evidence: The rim height of the sink or top of the counter may not be greater than 875 mm when measured from the surface to the ﬁ nished ﬂ oor.

requirements.label: Lavatory Pipe Offset and Insulation
requirements.description: Drain pipes beneath the lavatory should be offset and insulated to permit the person in a wheelchair to maneuver as far in as possible without burning limbs.
requirements.evidence: The drain pipes beneath the lavatory should be oę set and insulated to permit the person in a wheelchair to manoeuvre as far in as possible without burning limbs

requirements.label: Water Temperature Limiting
requirements.description: The temperature of the water from faucets shall be limited to a maximum of 49°C.
requirements.evidence: the temperature of the water from faucets shall be limited to a maximum of 49°C.

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Shelf Height
requirements.description: A shelf must be located not more than 1,200 mm above the floor in universal toilet rooms.
requirements.evidence: a shelf located not more than 1 200 mm above the ﬂ oor

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Water Closet Clearance
requirements.description: Water closet must have a clearance to the walls of 285–305 mm on one side and not less than 875 mm on the other side.
requirements.evidence: has a clearance to the walls of i) not less than 285 mm and not more than 305 mm on one side, and ii) not less than 875 mm on the other side

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Wheelchair Back-in Space
requirements.description: Universal toilet rooms must be designed to permit a wheelchair to back in alongside the water closet.
requirements.evidence: be designed to permit a wheelchair to back in alongside the water closet in the space referred to in Subclause (d) (ii)

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Door Closer
requirements.description: If the door is outward-swinging, it must have a door closer, spring hinges, or gravity hinges so that the door closes automatically.
requirements.evidence: if it is an outward-swinging door, a door closer, spring hinges or gravity hinges, so that the door closes automatically

requirements.label: Universal Toilet Room Attendant Accommodation
requirements.description: Universal toilet rooms must accommodate a user and a family member or attendant of the opposite gender for private hygiene.
requirements.evidence: A universal toilet room is required to accommodate a user and a family member or an attendant of the opposite gender so that hygiene may be carried out with dignity and in private.

requirements.label: Grab Bar Backing and Strength
requirements.description: Backing for grab bar installation must be at least a 19 mm sheet of plywood or reasonable alternative, floor to ceiling, to ensure grab bars resist a load of 1.3 kN (132 kg) applied vertically or horizontally.
requirements.evidence: SuĚ  cient backing (at least a 19 mm sheet of plywood or reasonable alternative behind cement board, ﬂ oor to ceiling, is preferred for optimum strength and placement) is required for the installation of grab bars...Grab bars shall resist a load of 1.3 kN (132 kg) applied vertically or horizontally

requirements.label: Grab Bar Dimensions and Placement
requirements.description: Grab bars must be 30–40 mm in diameter, with 35–45 mm clearance from the wall, and mounted as specified beside and behind the water closet.
requirements.evidence: be not less than 30 mm and not more than 40 mm in diameter, and e) have a clearance of not less than 35 mm and not more than 45 mm from the wall

requirements.label: Accessible Shower Stall Dimensions
requirements.description: The inside measurements of a wheelchair-accessible shower stall shall be a minimum of 1 500 mm in length and a minimum of 900 mm in depth.
requirements.evidence: The inside measurements of a wheelchair-accessible shower stall shall be a minimum of 1 500 mm in length and a minimum of 900 mm in depth

requirements.label: Accessible Change Cubicle Dimensions
requirements.description: The inside measurement of a change cubicle should be at least 2 000 mm × 2 000 mm with slip-resistant flooring and equipped with a wall-mounted bench seat.
requirements.evidence: The inside measurement of a change cubicle should be at least 2 000 mm × 2 000 mm with slip-resistant ﬂ ooring and equipped with a wall-mounted bench seat

requirements.label: Grab Bar Load Resistance
requirements.description: Grab bars that are installed shall resist a load not less than 1.3 kN applied vertically or horizontally.
requirements.evidence: Grab bars that are installed shall resist a load not less than 1.3 kN applied vertically or horizontally.

requirements.label: Accessible Controls Mounting Height
requirements.description: Controls for building services or safety devices, including electrical switches, panels, thermostats, and intercoms, shall be mounted between 400 mm and 1 200 mm above the finished floor.
requirements.evidence: Controls for the operation of building services or safety devices...shall be mounted between 400 mm and 1 200 mm above the ﬁ nished ﬂ oor.

requirements.label: Accessible Electrical Outlet Height
requirements.description: Electrical outlets shall be mounted between 400 mm and 1 200 mm above the finished floor level.
requirements.evidence: Electrical outlets shall be mounted between 400 mm and 1 200 mm above the ﬁ nished ﬂ oor level.

requirements.label: Accessible Window Controls
requirements.description: Window opening devices shall be located not more than 60 mm above the window sill and not require tight grasping, pinching, or twisting of the wrist.
requirements.evidence: opening devices located not more than 60 mm above the window sill and of a design that does not require tight grasping, pinching or twisting of the wrist

values.text: not more than 60 mm above sill

requirements.label: Accessible Window Sill Height
requirements.description: Window sills shall be located so that the sill is not more than 865 mm above the floor level.
requirements.evidence: be located so that the sill is not more than 865 mm above the ﬂ oor level

requirements.label: Control Height (Thermostats, etc.)
requirements.description: Controls such as thermostats should be mounted at 1400 mm from the finished floor (measured to the centre line of the plate).
requirements.evidence: Controls, like thermostats should be mounted at 1 400 mm when measured from the centre line of the plate to the finished floor.

requirements.label: Colour Contrast for Doors
requirements.description: Provide colour contrast between the door trim, door, and wall for better visibility.
requirements.evidence: Colour contrast between the door trim, door and wall.

requirements.label: Tactile Differentiation for Wayfinding
requirements.description: Effective tactile differentiation between living areas should be provided for wayfinding and safety.
requirements.evidence: Effective tactile-differentiation between living areas for wayfinding and safety.

requirements.label: Lever-Handled Door Hardware
requirements.description: Lever-handled hardware should be used on all doors that latch.
requirements.evidence: Lever-handled hardware on all doors that latch.

requirements.label: Minimum Hallway Width
requirements.description: A minimum width of 1200 mm is needed for hallways to allow movement of a person who uses a wheelchair.
requirements.evidence: A minimum width of 1 200 mm is needed for movement of a person who uses a wheelchair.

requirements.label: Window Sill Height (Living/Dining)
requirements.description: Window sills in living and dining rooms should be no higher than 600 mm from the finished floor.
requirements.evidence: window sill in living and dining rooms no higher than 600 mm.

requirements.label: Window Sill Height (Bedroom/Bathroom/Other)
requirements.description: Window sills in bedrooms, bathrooms, and other rooms should be no higher than 750 mm from the finished floor.
requirements.evidence: window sills should be no higher than 750 mm when measured from the finished floor.

requirements.label: Window Control Height
requirements.description: Window controls should be placed 750 mm to 1000 mm from the floor.
requirements.evidence: window controls should be placed in an area 750 mm to 1 000 mm when measured from the floor.

requirements.label: Bariatric Accommodation (Doorways and Hallways)
requirements.description: For bariatric accommodation, doorways should have a clear width opening of 1100 mm - 1200 mm, and hallways should be 1200 mm – 1500 mm in width.
requirements.evidence: doorways...should have a clear width opening of 1100 mm - 1200 mm, and hallways should be 1200 mm – 1500 mm in width

requirements.label: Kitchen Counter Heights and Knee Space
requirements.description: Counter tops should have at least two heights (915 mm and 760 mm) with knee space beneath. Height-adjustable cooktop and sink recommended.
requirements.evidence: Counter tops with at least two counter heights (915 mm and 760 mm) and with knee space beneath. Height-adjustable cook top...height-adjustable sink

requirements.label: Bathroom Transfer Space
requirements.description: A transfer space of 900 - 1000 mm located beside the toilet is required for wheelchair users.
requirements.evidence: A transfer space (for those who use wheelchairs) of 900 - 1 000 mm located beside the toilet.

requirements.label: Shower Size and Lip
requirements.description: Showers should be 1500 x 1500 mm with no lip for barrier-free access.
requirements.evidence: 1 500 x 1 500 mm shower with no lip

requirements.label: Grab Bars in Bathrooms
requirements.description: Grab bars should be provided for the toilet, shower, and bathtub.
requirements.evidence: Grab bars for the toilet, shower and bathtub.

requirements.label: Tactile Warning Strips at Elevation Changes
requirements.description: A downward change in elevation in a barrier-free path of travel shall be signalled by a 600 mm wide tactile warning strip placed 250 mm from the edge and for the full width, identified using colour and brightness contrast.
requirements.evidence: a downward change in elevation shall be signalled by the use of a 600 mm wide tactile warning strip placed 250 mm from the edge and for the full width of a stair, escalator, moving walkway, ramp or platform, and identiﬁ ed using colour and brightness contrast.

requirements.label: Balcony Refuge Dimensions
requirements.description: A balcony required as a refuge must have direct barrier-free access, be at least 1.5 m deep, and provide at least 1.5 m² per nonambulatory occupant and 0.5 m² per ambulatory occupant.
requirements.evidence: be not less than 1.5 m deep from the outside face of the exterior wall to the inside edge of the balcony, and provide not less than 1.5 m² of balcony space for each nonambulatory occupant and 0.5 m² for each ambulatory occupant.

requirements.label: Grab Bars in Bathtubs
requirements.description: Grab bars must be installed in every bathing unit, whether designated accessible or not, to provide safety and accommodate guests needing moderate assistance.
requirements.evidence: Grab bars must be installed in every bathing unit, whether designated accessible or not.

requirements.label: Bathtub Clear Floor Space and Grab Bar Placement
requirements.description: Bathtubs must have a clear floor space at least 900 mm wide along its length, and grab bars 1200 mm long located vertically and horizontally at specified heights above the rim.
requirements.evidence: have a clear ﬂ oor space at least 900 mm wide along its length, ... grab bars that ... are 1 200 mm long located vertically at the end ... with the lower end between 180 mm and 280 mm above the bathtub rim, and ... are 1 200 mm long located horizontally along the length ... at 180 mm to 280 mm above the bathtub rim

requirements.label: Fire Alarm Sound Pressure Levels
requirements.description: Fire alarm signal sound pressure level must not exceed 110 dBA in any normally occupied area; in sleeping rooms, not less than 75 dBA with doors closed.
requirements.evidence: The ﬁ re alarm signal sound pressure level shall be not more than 110 dBA ... The sound pressure level in a sleeping room ... shall be not less than 75 dBA ... doors ... closed.

requirements.label: Alarm Sound Level Above Ambient
requirements.description: Fire alarm audible signal devices must be at least 10 dBA above ambient noise, and not less than 65 dBA.
requirements.evidence: sound pressure level from a ﬁ re alarm system’s audible signal device ... shall be not less than 10 dBA above the ambient noise level without being less than 65 dBA.

requirements.label: Manual Silencing of Audible Signal Devices
requirements.description: Audible signal devices in dwelling units must include a means for manual silencing for up to 10 minutes, after which they restore to normal operation.
requirements.evidence: audible signal devices located within a dwelling unit shall include a means for them to be manually silenced for a period of not more than 10 min, after which time the devices shall restore themselves to normal operation.

requirements.label: Visual Signal Device Installation
requirements.description: Visual signal devices must be installed in addition to audible devices in buildings with fire alarm systems, conforming to CAN/ULC-S526, and be visible within suites.
requirements.evidence: Visual signal devices shall be installed in addition to audible signal devices in buildings required to have a ﬁ re alarm system and shall conform to CAN/ULC-S526 ... so that the signal from at least one device is visible within a suite

requirements.label: Vibration Devices for Deaf/Hard of Hearing
requirements.description: Suites for persons who are deaf, hard of hearing, or deaf-blind should be equipped with visual signalling devices and a vibration device for the bed.
requirements.evidence: Suites designated for use by persons who are deaf, hard of hearing or deaf-blind should also be equipped with visual signalling devices and a vibration device for the bed

requirements.label: Tactile Warning Strip Placement and Contrast
requirements.description: Tactile warning strips must begin 850 mm back from the edge, be full width, feel/sound different, and be in contrasting colour to surrounding flooring.
requirements.evidence: The required tactile warning strip shall begin 850 mm back from the edge in question and for its full width. It should feel and/or sound noticeably dię erent ... and be in a contrasting colour

requirements.label: Downward Elevation Change Warning
requirements.description: A 600 mm wide tactile warning strip, placed 250 mm from the edge, must signal a downward change in elevation in a barrier-free path of travel.
requirements.evidence: a downward change in elevation shall be signalled by the use of a 600 mm wide tactile warning strip placed 250 mm from the edge and for the full width

requirements.label: Cane-Detectable Partition at Telephones
requirements.description: Public telephones or banks of telephones should retain a cane-detectable partition for people with visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: should retain a cane-detectable partition in order for a person with visual disabilities to detect that s/he is approaching an area with projecting shelves and telephones.

requirements.label: TTY/TDD Public Telephone Availability
requirements.description: At least one telephone with a built-in telecommunication device for the deaf (TTY/TDD) shall be provided if public telephones are installed.
requirements.evidence: At least one telephone with a built-in telecommunication device for the deaf (TTY/TDD) shall be provided if public telephones are installed.

requirements.label: TTY Signage in Large Complexes
requirements.description: In large complexes with more than one bank of pay telephones, signs should indicate the location of the TTY.
requirements.evidence: where more than one bank of pay telephones is provided, signs should indicate the location of the TTY.

requirements.label: Assistive Listening System in Assembly Areas
requirements.description: All assembly areas over 100 m² must be equipped with an assistive listening system encompassing the entire seating area, except induction loop systems may cover half.
requirements.evidence: all assembly areas with an area of more than 100 m² shall be equipped with an assistive listening system encompassing the entire seating area.

requirements.label: Assistive Listening Devices for 50+ Occupancy
requirements.description: Assistive Listening Devices (ALD) should be installed in all areas of any building where occupancy might be 50 persons and more.
requirements.evidence: Assistive Listening Devices (ALD) should be installed in all areas of any building where occupancy might be 50 persons and more.

requirements.label: Annual ALD System Check
requirements.description: All Assisted Listening Systems should be checked once per year to ensure proper operation.
requirements.evidence: All Assisted Listening Systems should be checked once per year to ensure proper operation.

requirements.label: Induction Loop Receiver Provision
requirements.description: Where an induction loop system is deployed, the building should have 1 loop receiver for every 50 occupants.
requirements.evidence: the building should have 1 loop receiver for every 50 occupants

requirements.label: Induction Loop System Certification
requirements.description: Installation of induction loop shall comply with IEC60118-4 2014 standard, and a Certificate of Conformity should be issued; checked annually.
requirements.evidence: Installation of induction loop shall comply with the IEC60118-4 2014 standard, and a Certiﬁ cate of Conformity should be issued...Once per year, each loop installation should be checked and a Certiﬁ cate of Conformity shall be issued.

requirements.label: Magnetic Counter Loop in Health Care
requirements.description: Health care locations manned by staff should be equipped with a magnetic counter loop system and the 't coil' symbol should be displayed.
requirements.evidence: Heath Care locations manned by staę  should be equipped with a magnetic counter loop system and the “t coil” symbol should be displayed.

requirements.label: Tactile Signage Requirements
requirements.description: Tactile signage must be not less than 60 mm high, raised ~0.7 mm, located ≤1200 mm above floor, ≤150 mm from entrance, and contrasting in colour.
requirements.evidence: be not less than 60 mm high, raised approximately 0.7 mm above the surface, be located not more than 1 200 mm above the ﬁ nished ﬂ oor, begin not more than 150 mm from the door or entrance, be contrasting in colour

values.text: 60 mm high, 0.7 mm raised, ≤1200 mm height, ≤150 mm from entrance

requirements.label: Multilingual Signage
requirements.description: Standards must be available in multiple formats, including plain-language, ASL, and LSQ summaries, to ensure accessibility for diverse users.
requirements.evidence: providing standards in multiple formats, including plain-language, American Sign language (ASL) and langue des signes québécoise (LSQ) summaries

alternates.label: Multilingual Signage
alternates.description: Where wording is used on signs, language should be simple and concise to be accessible for most building users.
alternates.evidence: if wording is to be used on signs, the language on them should be simple and concise to be accessible for most building users.

requirements.label: Covered Way Minimum Dimensions and Features
requirements.description: Covered ways at construction sites must have a clear height ≥2.5m, clear width ≥1.5m, weathertight roof, smooth enclosure, adequate lighting, and lockable gates.
requirements.evidence: A covered way shall a) have a clear height of not less than 2.5 m, b) have a clear width of not less than 1.5 m...g) be adequately lighted...h) have...a gate not less than 1 200 mm high...

requirements.label: Obstruction-Free Temporary Routes
requirements.description: Temporary sidewalks and adjacent sidewalks must be kept clear of obstructions at all times; no materials, scrap, or equipment on walking surfaces.
requirements.evidence: where a sidewalk exists adjacent to the site, it shall be kept clear of obstructions at all times...Materials, scrap, and equipment cannot be placed on the walking surface as this poses a hazard for all users.

requirements.label: Cane-Detectable Zone
requirements.description: Fences and covered ways must not have objects protruding beyond the cane-detectable zone to ensure safety for people with visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: Care is called for in erecting fences and covered ways so that no objects protrude beyond the cane-detectable zone.

requirements.label: Advance Notice and Intelligible Signage
requirements.description: Provide intelligible signage with symbols, arrows, and large print in contrasting colours to give advance notice of closures, detours, or delays.
requirements.evidence: Use symbols, arrows, and large-sized print text in contrasting colours identified with safety and danger...Advance notice—time and positional—results in more-informed decisions by all users...

requirements.label: Accessible Washroom in Outdoor Areas
requirements.description: Where washrooms are provided in outdoor public use eating areas, at least one universal toilet room per cluster of regular washrooms must be accessible.
requirements.evidence: ensure accessible features (e.g., at least one universal toilet room per cluster of regular washrooms).

requirements.label: Accessible Trailhead Signage
requirements.description: Designated trailheads must include information signage at key entrance, exit, and decision points.
requirements.evidence: ensure that designated trailheads with information signage are integrated as part of the trail design at key entrance and exit points along the trail, intermediate areas on lengthy trails, or decision points

requirements.label: Trail Surface Stability and Durability
requirements.description: Trail surfaces must be firm, stable, resistant to weather, and able to sustain typical wear and tear between maintenance cycles.
requirements.evidence: ensure that surface is ﬁ rm and stable; ensure that surface is resistant to damage by normal weather conditions, with ability to sustain typical wear and tear between planned maintenance cycles;

requirements.label: Trail Slope Requirements
