requirements.description: Provide running and cross slopes as gentle as possible, as permitted by terrain, to minimize user effort and provide even surfaces.
requirements.evidence: provide a running slope that is as gentle as possible, as permitted by the terrain, to minimize amount of strength and stamina required to use the trail; and ensure that cross slopes are as gentle as possible, as permitted by the terrain, to provide an even surface for diverse users

requirements.label: Edge Protection for Trails Adjacent to Hazards
requirements.description: Where trails are adjacent to water or drop-offs, provide edge protection: elevated barrier, top at least 50 mm above trail, not impeding drainage.
requirements.evidence: provide edge protection with the following requirements: constitute of an elevated barrier ... top of the edge protection at 50 mm (minimum) high above the trail surface; ... not to impede the drainage

requirements.label: Trailhead Signage Content and Contrast
requirements.description: Trailhead signage must include trail length, surface type, width, slopes, features, and have high tonal contrast with sans serif font.
requirements.evidence: provide signage with the following information ... ensure that signage text has high tonal contrast with its background in order to assist with visual recognition, with text that includes characters that use a sans serif font.

requirements.label: Accessible Trail Information in Other Media
requirements.description: Where other media (websites, brochures) provide trail information, include the same information as trailhead signage.
requirements.evidence: where other media such as park websites or brochures are used to provide information about the recreational trail ... provide the same information identiﬁ ed on the trailhead signage.

requirements.label: Edge Protection on Boardwalks
requirements.description: Provide edge protection that is 50 mm (minimum) high on boardwalks; design must allow suitable drainage.
requirements.evidence: provide edge protection that is 50 mm (minimum) high; and ... design allows suitable drainage

requirements.label: Accessible Trailhead Amenities
requirements.description: Exterior amenities (e.g., parking, accessible routes, public washrooms) should be provided at trailheads and reviewed for accessibility compliance.
requirements.evidence: Are EXTERIOR AMENITIES provided at trailheads (e.g., parking, accessible routes, public washrooms, etc.)?

requirements.label: Trail Clear Width and Obstruction-Free
requirements.description: Trail clear width must be at least 1000 mm (1800 mm preferred); vegetation and features must not obstruct accessible route.
requirements.evidence: Is the CLEAR WIDTH of the trail at least 1000 mm (1800 mm preferred)? ... does not create obstruction or projection along accessible route.

requirements.label: Trail Edge Protection and Marking
requirements.description: Where there are changes in level, provide edge protection at least 50 mm high and clearly marked (e.g., colour and texture contrast).
requirements.evidence: are EDGE PROTECTION at least 50 mm high provided and edges clearly marked (e.g., colour and texture contrast)

requirements.label: Trail Slope
requirements.description: Running and cross slopes should be as gentle as possible, as permitted by the terrain.
requirements.evidence: Is the RUNNING SLOPE as gentle as possible, as permitted by the terrain?

requirements.label: Rest Areas and Trail Experience Options
requirements.description: Trail design should offer areas for rest and options for shorter or longer on-trail adventures to suit user preferences.
requirements.evidence: Does the trail oę er areas for rest and options for shorter or longer on-trail adventures so that trail users can choose the experience that most suit them?

requirements.label: Trail Maintenance Policy
requirements.description: A policy should be in place to address maintenance issues for year-round trails (e.g., removal of debris and obstructions).
requirements.evidence: Is there a policy in place to address maintenance issues for trails designed for year-round use (e.g., removal of debris and obstructions on trail surfaces etc)?

requirements.label: Inclusive Play Space Consultation
requirements.description: Consultation with the public, persons with disabilities, and Accessibility Advisory Committee is required when constructing or redeveloping outdoor play spaces.
requirements.evidence: consultation on the needs of children and caregivers with various disabilities must occur with: a) the public and persons with disabilities; and b) your local Accessibility Advisory Committee

requirements.label: Inclusive Play Space Features
requirements.description: Incorporate accessibility features, such as sensory and active play components, for children and caregivers with various disabilities into outdoor play space design.
requirements.evidence: incorporate accessibility features, such as sensory and active play components, for children and caregivers with various disabilities into the design of outdoor play spaces;

requirements.label: Inclusive Play Space Standards Compliance
requirements.description: Design of inclusive play spaces and features must meet requirements of CAN/CSA Z614-14, Annex H.
requirements.evidence: Ensure that the design of inclusive play spaces and features meet the requirements of CAN / CSA Z614-14, Annex H

requirements.label: Accessible Play Space Entry/Exit Points
requirements.description: Provide a minimum of two accessible ingress/egress points, located as part of an adjacent accessible route, with firm, stable, slip-resistant connections.
requirements.evidence: Provide a minimum of two accessible ingress/egress points: a) that are located as part of an adjacent accessible route; b) that ensure that accessible connections provided to play space surfaces are ﬁ rm, stable, and slip-resistant

requirements.label: Transfer Systems for Play Components
requirements.description: Provide transfer systems (e.g., transfer steps or platforms) to connect elevated or ground-level play components, with a minimum clear floor space of 915 mm wide by 1370 mm long adjacent to all transfer locations.
requirements.evidence: provide transfer systems to connect elevated or ground-level play components (e.g., transfer steps or platforms); ... provide a minimum clear ﬂ oor space of 915 mm wide by 1370 mm long adjacent to all transfer locations

values.text: 915 mm x 1370 mm min clear floor space

requirements.label: High Tonal Contrast for Play Components
requirements.description: Provide a high tonal contrast between a play component and its surroundings.
requirements.evidence: provide a high tonal contrast between a play component and its surroundings.

requirements.label: Bariatric Toilet and Shower Accommodation
requirements.description: Bathrooms must use bariatric floor-mounted toilets rated to 450 kg, provide minimum 530 mm between toilet and wall, and showers at least 1,500 mm × 1,500 mm.
requirements.evidence: Use bariatric ﬂoor-mounted toilets that are rated to 450 kg...minimum distance of 530 mm between the toilet and the ﬂanking wall...private showers are at least 1 500 mm × 1 500 mm.

requirements.label: Reinforced Grab Bars for Bariatric Use
requirements.description: Wall-mounted grab bars must be structurally reinforced and rated to handle 4.5 kN (454 kg), and mounted further forward from the back wall.
requirements.evidence: Ensure that wall-mounted grab bars are structurally reinforced and rated to handle 4.5 kN (454 kg). They should also be mounted further forward from the back wall.

requirements.label: Shower Accessibility for Bariatric Users
requirements.description: Showers must avoid enclosing walls, use curtains, include multiple reinforced grab bars, removable shower head, and avoid curbs.
requirements.evidence: Avoid enclosing walls; instead, use shower curtains...multiple, reinforced grab bars...removable shower head...Avoid curbs into accessible showers.

requirements.label: Turning Radius for Wheelchairs and Caregivers
requirements.description: Allow a larger turning radius for individuals using wheelchairs; recommended 1,900 mm for wheelchairs or commodes and a caregiver.
requirements.evidence: A recommended 1 900 mm turning radius for wheelchairs or commodes and a caregiver is suggested.

requirements.label: Barrier-Free Safe Path of Travel
requirements.description: Provide a barrier-free safe path of travel, accessible entrances (preferably at zero grade with powered doors).
requirements.evidence: require ease of access to buildings and all facilities and services. This includes a barrier-free safe path of travel, accessible entrances (preferably at zero grade with powered doors)

requirements.label: Handrail Load Requirements
requirements.description: Handrails must withstand a minimum of 4.5 kN (454 kg) at any point and a uniform load of 3.3 kN (340 kg) in any direction outside dwelling units.
requirements.evidence: Recommend construction to withstand a minimum of 4.5 kN (454 kg) applied at any point and in any direction and a uniform load of 3.3 kN (340 kg) applied in any direction to handrails

requirements.label: Bedroom Clearance for Bariatric Users
requirements.description: Provide 1,500 mm of clearance around the bed for caregiver assistance, equipment, and lifts.
requirements.evidence: Have 1 500 mm of clearance available around the bed of an obese individual to allow room for caregiver assistance, equipment such as wheelchairs and walkers, and space for lifts

requirements.label: Grab Bar Load Rating
requirements.description: Grab bars must be rated for at least 454 kg and placed strategically from bed to toilet.
requirements.evidence: Have grab bars rated for at least 454 kg placed strategically from bed to toilet

requirements.label: Space for Oversized Equipment
requirements.description: Provide space for oversized furniture and storage of oversized equipment (e.g., 1 sq m for a portable lift, 370 sq m for a walker, 1.5 sq m for a wheelchair, 465 sq mm for a commode).
requirements.evidence: Consider space for oversized furniture and storage of oversized equipment (i.e., 1 sq m for a portable lift, 370 sq m for a walker, 1.5 sq m for a wheelchair, 465 sq mm for a commode).

requirements.label: Personal Space Temperature Control
requirements.description: Provide strategies for temperature control in personal spaces, such as increased air conditioning, access to controls, and/or fans.
requirements.evidence: Recommend strategies to help them control temperature in personal spaces, e.g., increase air conditioning capacity, allow access to temperature controls, and/or add a ceiling fan or portable fan.

requirements.label: Elevator Door Reopening Device
requirements.description: Elevator doors must have a reopening device that stops and reopens the door to at least 910 mm if obstructed, sensing objects at 125 mm ±25 and 735 mm ±25 mm above the floor.
requirements.evidence: Doors shall be provided with a door-reopening device that will function to stop and reopen a car door and an adjacent landing door to at least 910 mm, in case the car door is obstructed while closing. This reopening device shall also be capable of sensing an object or person in the path of a closing door at a nominal 125 mm ±25 and 735 mm ±25 mm above the floor without requiring contact for activation.

requirements.label: Elevator Door Timing
requirements.description: Elevator doors must remain open at least 5 seconds for hall calls and 3 seconds for car calls before closing.
requirements.evidence: a minimum period of 5 seconds shall elapse before the doors start to close if it is a hall call, and 3 seconds if it is a car call.

requirements.label: Elevator Car Controls Clear Floor Space
requirements.description: A clear floor space of 760 mm x 1,220 mm minimum must be provided at elevator controls.
requirements.evidence: A clear floor space of 760 mm × 1 220 mm minimum shall be provided at the controls.

requirements.label: Elevator Car Control Heights
requirements.description: Elevator floor buttons must be a maximum of 1,220 mm above the floor; emergency controls grouped at the bottom, with centre lines at least 890 mm above the floor.
requirements.evidence: Buttons with floor designations shall be located a maximum of 1 220 mm above the floor...Emergency control buttons have their centre lines 890 mm minimum above the floor

requirements.label: Elevator Car Inside Dimensions
requirements.description: Elevator car inside dimensions must comply with Table E-1 (referenced as based on ANSI/ICC A117.1), with a tolerance of minus 16 mm.
requirements.evidence: The inside dimensions of elevator cars shall comply with Table E-1...A tolerance of minus 16 mm shall be permitted.

requirements.label: Elevator Self-Levelling
requirements.description: Elevators must have a self-levelling feature that maintains the car at floor landings within ±13 mm under all loading conditions.
requirements.evidence: Each car shall be equipped with a self-levelling feature that will automatically bring and maintain the car at floor landings within a tolerance of ±13 mm under rated loading to zero loading conditions.

requirements.label: Elevator Door Reopening Device Duration
requirements.description: Elevator door-reopening devices must remain effective for at least 20 seconds.
requirements.evidence: Door-reopening devices shall remain effective for a period of not less than 20 seconds.

requirements.label: Handrail Height and Clearance
requirements.description: Handrails shall be provided on all non-access walls at 800 mm to 920 mm height, with 35 mm to 45 mm clearance from wall.
requirements.evidence: Handrails shall be provided on all non-access walls. The top of the gripping surfaces of the handrails shall be at a height of 800 mm to 920 mm, with a space of 35 mm to 45 mm between the handrails and wall.

requirements.label: Visible Signal Size and Height
requirements.description: Visible signal elements at hall entrances must be at least 60 mm in smallest dimension and 1,830 mm minimum above floor.
requirements.evidence: Hall ﬁxture signals shall be 1 830 mm minimum above the ﬂoor ... The visible signal elements shall be 60 mm minimum in the smallest dimension.

values.text: 60 mm min size, 1,830 mm min height

requirements.label: Tactile Elevator Car Identification
requirements.description: A tactile elevator car identification shall be placed immediately below the hoistway entrance floor designation; characters shall be 50 mm high.
requirements.evidence: a tactile elevator car identiﬁcation shall be placed immediately below the hoistway entrance ﬂoor designation. The characters shall be 50 mm high

requirements.label: Accessible Operating Controls
requirements.description: All rooms and spaces must have operating controls in accordance with Clauses 13.1 to 13.9, supporting future accessibility.
requirements.evidence: have operating controls in accordance with Clauses 13.1 to 13.9

requirements.label: Headroom Clearance
requirements.description: Maintain headroom clearances in accordance with Clause 12.3.6 to ensure accessible movement throughout the dwelling.
requirements.evidence: maintain headroom clearances in accordance with Clause 12.3.6

requirements.label: Accessible Lighting Design
requirements.description: Design for accessible-ready (DAR) must accommodate illumination in accordance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.8, allowing for future accessible lighting installation.
requirements.evidence: be designed to accommodate illumination in accordance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.8

requirements.label: Accessible Landscaping for Future Features
requirements.description: Landscaping on accessible-ready exterior paths must be removable with standard hand tools and not require a permit for removal.
requirements.evidence: be removable using standard hand tools (e.g., gardening tools, shovels, picks, crowbars, etc.); and ii) not require a permit for removal or demolition of the landscaping (e.g. trees).

requirements.label: Exterior Stairs Compliance
requirements.description: Exterior stairs must be designed and constructed in compliance with clause 5.6 of CSA B652.
requirements.evidence: Exterior stairs shall be designed and constructed in compliance with clause 5.6 of CSA B652.

requirements.label: Accessible Parking for Shared Amenities
requirements.description: Shared parking amenities must provide assigned accessible parking per CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.3.2, or garages per 5.3.3.
requirements.evidence: assigned accessible parking in accordance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.3.2, or garages designed in accordance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.3.3 must be provided.

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Parking Spaces
requirements.description: Parking spaces serving only an accessible-ready house must be designed per CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.3.1 and 5.3.2, allowing area allowances without destructive modifications.
requirements.evidence: parking spaces serving only an accessible-ready house shall be designed in accordance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.3.1 and clause 5.3.2.

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Parking Garage Dimensions
requirements.description: Parking garages or carports serving only an accessible-ready house must have a driveway at least 4600 mm wide by 8600 mm long, or comply with CSA/ASC B652 5.3.3 and 5.3.4.
requirements.evidence: a parking space (i.e. driveway) leading up to the parking garage or carport that is a minimum of 4600 mm wide by 8600 mm long; or b) be designed in accordance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.3.3 and clause 5.3.4.

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Interior Spaces
requirements.description: Accessible-ready houses must have at least one accessible-ready bathroom, kitchen, bedroom, laundry room, and entry/dining/living room with accessible path of travel.
requirements.evidence: Accessible-ready houses shall be designed such that they have at least: a) one bathroom in accordance with Clause 12.3.10; ... e) if present, one entry way, dining room, living room served with a path of travel in accordance with Clause 12.3.3.

requirements.label: Accessible Maneuvering Area at Doors
requirements.description: Doorways must have a level, clear, and unobstructed maneuvering area extending specified distances beyond the latch-side and path of travel.
requirements.evidence: have a level, clear, and unobstructed maneuvering area that extends for the full height of the door and is not less than: 600 mm beyond the latch-side on the pull side; 300 mm beyond the latch-side on the push side; ... not less than 1200 mm for interior spaces and not less than 1500 mm for exterior spaces.

values.text: 600 mm (pull side), 300 mm (push side), 1200 mm (interior), 1500 mm (exterior)

requirements.label: Accessible Two Doors in Series
requirements.description: Where two doors are in series, design must allow for removal of one door or achieving required distance without major modifications to building systems.
requirements.evidence: be designed such that one of the doors can be removed without significant modifications for the structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Path and Space Modifications
requirements.description: Walls that may need to be moved for accessible paths or spaces must be non-loadbearing, with easily terminable electrical, and no plumbing or HVAC.
requirements.evidence: requiring walls that may need to be moved to achieve the space requirement to: 1) be non-load bearing walls ... 2) have electrical systems that can be terminated at a nearby junction box ... 3) have no plumbing in them, and 4) have no HVAC in them.

requirements.label: Accessible Door Hardware Compatibility
requirements.description: Doors must be designed to accept hardware that complies with accessibility standards, allowing for easy replacement.
requirements.evidence: Doors shall be designed and constructed to accept door hardware that complies with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.7.5.

requirements.label: Accessible Door Thresholds
requirements.description: Door thresholds must comply with accessibility requirements as specified in referenced standards.
requirements.evidence: Thresholds shall be as required by CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.7.4.

requirements.label: Accessible Door-Opening Force
requirements.description: Door openings must be designed to accept doors that comply with accessible door-opening force requirements.
requirements.evidence: The design and the construction of door openings shall accept doors that comply with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.7.6.

requirements.label: Accessible Door Vision Panel Compatibility
requirements.description: Door openings must be compatible with doors or sidelights that have vision panels meeting accessibility standards.
requirements.evidence: the door opening shall be compatible to receive a door where the vision panel in the door or the sidelight comply with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.7.9.

requirements.label: Accessible Counter Backsplash Height
requirements.description: Where present, counter backsplashes must start from a height of 730 mm above the finished floor.
requirements.evidence: where present, have the backsplash start from a height of 730 mm above the finished floor.

requirements.label: Protruding Objects
requirements.description: All protrusions must comply with CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.7.2 or be removable without affecting integral building systems.
requirements.evidence: All protrusions shall: a) comply with the limitations and requirements of CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.7.2; or b) be removable without affecting the integral buildings systems...

requirements.label: Accessible Wall Finishes
requirements.description: Wall finishes must be installed per CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.6 or allow for future replacement to compliant finishes without major repairs.
requirements.evidence: Wall finishes, surfaces, and coverings shall: a) be installed in accordance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.6; or b) be installed: i) in a manner that allows their replacement...

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Bathroom
requirements.description: At least one bathroom in an accessible-ready house must be designed as an accessible-ready bathroom.
requirements.evidence: At least one bathroom in an accessible-ready house shall be designed as an accessible-ready bathroom.

requirements.label: Bathroom Turning Space
requirements.description: Turning space in the accessible-ready bathroom must comply with Clause 12.3.4 or be designed for future compliance without significant modifications.
requirements.evidence: The turning space within the accessible-ready bathroom...shall: a) comply with the requirements of Clause 12.3.4; or b) ...be designed such that it can be constructed meeting the requirements of sentence a) without significant modifications...

requirements.label: Bathroom Wall Reinforcing
requirements.description: Accessible-ready bathroom walls must comply with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.5 for reinforcement (e.g., for grab bars).
requirements.evidence: The accessible-ready bathroom walls shall comply with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.5.

requirements.label: Bathroom Lighting Compatibility
requirements.description: Accessible-ready bathroom lighting must be compatible with fixtures that comply with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.6.
requirements.evidence: The accessible-ready bathroom lighting shall be compatible with lighting fixtures that comply with the requirements of CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.6.

requirements.label: Bathroom Electrical Accessibility
requirements.description: Accessible-ready bathroom electrical must comply with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.8 or have conduits for future GFCI receptacle installation without major work.
requirements.evidence: The accessible-ready bathroom electrical shall: a) comply with the requirements of CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.8; or b) have electrical conduits between a power source and the locations where GFCI receptacles need to be located...

requirements.label: Bathroom Luminance (Colour) Contrast
requirements.description: Accessible-ready bathroom fixtures must be compatible for replacement with fixtures complying with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.9.
requirements.evidence: The accessible-ready bathroom fixtures shall be compatible to be replaced with fixtures complying with the CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.9.

requirements.label: Bathroom Toilet and Accessories
requirements.description: Accessible-ready bathroom toilet and accessories must comply with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.10 or be designed for future compliance without significant modifications.
requirements.evidence: The accessible-ready bathroom toilet and accessories shall: a) comply with the requirements of CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.10; or b) ...be designed such that it can be constructed meeting the requirements of sentence a) without significant modifications...

requirements.label: Bathroom Grab Bars
requirements.description: Where installed, bathroom grab bars must meet the requirements of CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.11.
requirements.evidence: Where installed, bathroom grab bars shall meet the requirements of CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.11.

requirements.label: Bathroom Sink and Vanity
requirements.description: Accessible-ready bathroom sink and vanity must comply with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.12 or be designed for future compliance without significant modifications.
requirements.evidence: The accessible-ready bathroom sink and vanity shall: a) comply with the requirements of CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.12; or b) ...be designed such that it can be constructed meeting the requirements of sentence a) without significant modifications...

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Bathroom Showers
requirements.description: Bathroom showers must be designed to allow future retrofit to CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.13 compliance without significant modifications to structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems.
requirements.evidence: The bathroom, including the shower, shall be designed such that it can be constructed meeting the requirements of sentence a) without significant modifications for the structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Bathroom Bathtubs
requirements.description: Bathroom bathtubs must be designed to allow future retrofit to CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.14 compliance without significant modifications to structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems.
requirements.evidence: The bathroom, including the bathtub, shall be designed such that it can be constructed meeting the requirements of sentence a) without significant modifications for the structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Bathroom Medicine Cabinets
requirements.description: Bathroom medicine cabinets must be designed to allow future retrofit to CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.9.15 compliance without significant modifications to structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems.
requirements.evidence: The bathroom, including the medicine cabinet, shall be designed such that it can be constructed meeting the requirements of sentence a) without significant modifications for the structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Bathroom Storage
requirements.description: Bathroom storage must comply with CSA/ASC B652 clauses 4.4 and 4.5 or be removable without significant modifications to structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems.
requirements.evidence: bathroom storage shall: a) comply with CSA/ASC B652 clauses 4.4 and 4.5; or b) be removable without significant modifications for the structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Kitchens
requirements.description: Kitchens must be designed to allow future retrofit to CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.10 compliance without significant modifications to structural, plumbing, or HVAC systems.
requirements.evidence: The kitchen shall be designed such that it can be constructed meeting the requirements of sentence (a) without significant modifications for the structural, plumbing, or HVAC systems

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Bedrooms
requirements.description: Bedrooms must be designed to allow future retrofit to CSA/ASC B652 clauses 5.11.1, 5.11.2, and 5.11.3 compliance without significant modifications to structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems.
requirements.evidence: The bedroom shall be designed such that it can be constructed meeting the requirements of sentence a) without significant modifications for the structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Laundry Rooms
requirements.description: Laundry rooms must be designed to allow future retrofit to CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.12 compliance without significant modifications to structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems.
requirements.evidence: The laundry room shall be designed such that it can be constructed meeting the requirements of sentence aa) without significant modifications for the structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems

requirements.label: Accessible-Ready Closets
requirements.description: Closets must comply with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.13, be removable, or be designed for future retrofit without significant modifications to structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems.
requirements.evidence: The closet shall be designed such that it can be constructed meeting the requirements of sentence a) without significant modifications for the structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC systems

requirements.label: Accessible Service Rooms and Spaces
requirements.description: Service rooms must be served by accessible paths of travel, clear floor space, and have equipment controls located per referenced clauses, except for items requiring professional servicing.
requirements.evidence: a) be served by a path of travel in accordance with Clause 12.3.3; b) be served by a clear floor space in accordance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.4.2; and c) have equipment controls located in accordance with Clause 10

requirements.label: Operating Controls Height and Reach
requirements.description: Operating controls must be installed between 400 mm and 1100 mm from the floor, or 900 mm to 1100 mm where reading a display is required; must allow relocation between 400 mm and 1500 mm.
requirements.evidence: be installed between 400 mm and 1100 mm from the floor, and between 900 mm and 1100 mm from the floor in situations where reading a display is required ... allow the centerline ... to be relocated ... between 400 mm and 1500 mm from the floor.

requirements.label: Operating Controls Forward/Side Approach
requirements.description: Operating controls must have clear forward or side approach space per CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.5.4, or be relocatable without rewiring.
requirements.evidence: be installed with the clear forward space is provided for forward or side approach in compliance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.5.4; or ... be installed in a manner where the operable control can be relocated without the need to rewire.

requirements.label: Replaceable Operating Controls
requirements.description: Controls must be installed to allow replacement with controls complying with CSA/ASC B652 clauses 4.5.5, 4.5.6, 4.5.7, and 4.5.8 without additional work or repairs.
requirements.evidence: Controls shall be installed in a manner which allows their replacement with controls complying with CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.5.5 a) and b) without requiring any additional work or repairs.

requirements.label: Operating Controls Luminance Contrast
requirements.description: Controls must be installed to allow replacement with controls complying with CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.5.9, or allow background to be painted for compliant contrast.
requirements.evidence: be installed in a manner which allows their replacement with controls complying with CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.5.9 ... or ... background can be painted to allow for the combination of control and background complying with CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.5.9.

requirements.label: Replaceable General and Task Lighting
requirements.description: General and task lighting must be installed to allow replacement with fixtures compliant with CSA/ASC B652 clauses 4.8.1 and 4.8.2 without repairs or changes to building systems.
requirements.evidence: General (ambient) lights shall be installed in a manner that allows for their replacement with built-in lighting fixtures in compliance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 4.8.1, without the need for any repairs or changes to the building systems.

requirements.label: Stair Tread and Riser Dimensions
requirements.description: Stair risers must not exceed 200 mm in height; runs must be at least 255 mm deep; nosing must not project more than 38 mm; radius of curvature at leading edge not more than 13 mm.
requirements.evidence: have risers not more than 200 mm high; have runs not less than 255 mm deep ... The nosing shall: a) project not more than 38 mm; ... have a radius of curvature at the leading edge of the tread not more than 13 mm;

values.text: riser ≤ 200 mm; run ≥ 255 mm; nosing ≤ 38 mm; radius ≤ 13 mm

requirements.label: Stair Handrail Accommodation
requirements.description: Stairs must be designed and constructed to accept handrails in compliance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.6.3.
requirements.evidence: Stairs shall be designed and constructed to accept handrails in compliance with CSA/ASC B652 clause 5.6.3.

requirements.label: Wall Reinforcement for Future Accessibility Features
requirements.description: Walls that may support future accessible features must be reinforced, with reinforcement material, location, intent, connection details, and load capacity clearly shown on drawings.
requirements.evidence: any walls that need to support future accessible features shall be reinforced to accommodate the relevant future modification. The structural or architectural drawings shall: a) clearly show the reinforcement material, location, and intent; b) identify the connection details of the reinforcement; and c) identify the loads that the reinforcement is designed to withstand relative to its intended use.

requirements.label: Floor Shafts and Plugs for Lifts
requirements.description: Where vertical travel requires future floor shafts/openings, structure must include slab/floor opening, plug supporting full floor loads, and detailed drawings for opening, plug, connections, loads, and removal method.
requirements.evidence: the structure shall be: a) designed with a slab/floor opening to accommodate the possible installation of the service; b) designed with a plug capable of supporting the full loads of the floor; c) shown on the drawings with: i) the opening structural details; ii) the plug structural details; iii) the connection details; iv) the loads that they are designed to withstand in all configurations, and v) the method to be used to remove the plug.

requirements.label: Accessible Parking and Garage
requirements.description: Parking spaces, garages, and carports must provide accessible-ready paths of travel and be free of permanent obstructions.
requirements.evidence: Parking and garage path of travel from main entrance and garage entrance ... Parking and garages, parking spaces ... Parking and garages, carport serving a single-family house

requirements.label: Accessible Site Plan
requirements.description: Site plans must show designated areas for future development that remain free from structural, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing features.
requirements.evidence: Provide a clear overall site plan to show designated areas for future development that are to remain free from structural, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing features.

requirements.label: Accessible Cabinetry and Built-ins
requirements.description: Cabinetry in kitchens, bathrooms, and laundry rooms must allow for clear floor space, knee and toe clearance, and be replaceable without major modifications.
requirements.evidence: Cabinetry areas that require clear floor space, knee and toe clearance for stationary working areas to comply with Clause 12.3.4, such as kitchens, bathrooms, laundry rooms, need not be indicated on the accessible-ready construction plan as long as cabinetry can be replaced with compliant items.
